cases.title: TS-36: Absence threshold notification sent to student and instructor after session close when unexcused rate exceeds 20%
preconditions: Student A enrolled in class HESD-2026-A / subject SWE-101 with assigned Instructor I
Four prior Closed sessions for Student A in SWE-101 with attendance: 3 Present, 1 unexcused Absent (25% rate before next session)
Fifth session created, opened Active, and Student A remains Pending (will become Absent on close)
Should-capability FR-16 enabled; default policy absence_threshold_percent = 20 per BR-05
Authenticated Instructor I session with session:write
steps: POST /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/close using Instructor I session cookie
Assert HTTP 200 and session status Closed with closedAt populated
Wait for asynchronous NotificationService.evaluateAbsenceThresholds to complete (poll or test hook)
GET /api/v1/notifications using Student A session cookie
Assert at least one notification with type AbsenceThresholdWarning referencing subjectCode SWE-101
Assert notification payload absenceRate > 0.20, threshold 0.20, unexcusedAbsenceCount and sessionCount reflect 2 Absent of 5 completed sessions
GET /api/v1/notifications using Instructor I session cookie
Assert Instructor I receives AbsenceThresholdWarning notification for Student A in SWE-101 per AC-16a
cases.expected: AC-16a and testing-plan TS-36 satisfied: in-app notification sent to affected student and assigned instructor when session closes and unexcused absence rate exceeds 20%

cases.title: Tag-specific e2e: absence threshold notification fires when unexcused rate exceeds 20% after session close
preconditions: Student B enrolled in subject HESD-W01 with 4 completed Closed sessions: 3 Present, 1 Absent (25% unexcused rate)
Active session for same class-subject with Student B Pending attendance
FR-16 Should-capability enabled in test environment
steps: POST /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/close to finalize fifth session with Student B Pending → Absent
Await threshold evaluation job triggered by SessionClosed event per WF-06 §7.1
GET /api/v1/notifications for Student B and assert AbsenceThresholdWarning notification created
Assert payload.unexcusedAbsenceCount === 2 and payload.sessionCount === 5 yielding absenceRate 0.40
GET /api/v1/notifications for assigned instructor and assert matching warning notification exists
Assert no duplicate notifications for same student-subject pair on idempotent re-evaluation
cases.expected: Tag-specific e2e hint satisfied: absence threshold notification fires when unexcused rate exceeds 20% upon session transition to Closed

cases.title: NotificationService.evaluateAbsenceThresholds creates Notification rows when rate exceeds policy after session close
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database with Student C enrolled in subject with ClassAssignment linking Instructor C
Five Closed attendance_records for Student C: 2 Absent, 3 Present; no Excused rows
Newly closed sessionId with Student C finalized Absent (sixth completed session, third Absent → 50% rate)
PolicySetting absence_threshold_percent = 20 seeded per database design §10
NotificationService wired per notifications module §2.7
steps: Invoke NotificationService.evaluateAbsenceThresholds(sessionId) after session close commit
Query notifications table for userId = Student C and type AbsenceThresholdWarning
Assert one notification row created with payload JSONB containing subjectCode, absenceRate, threshold, sessionCount, unexcusedAbsenceCount
Query notifications table for assigned Instructor C userId
Assert instructor notification row created referencing same student and subject
Assert absenceRate = unexcusedAbsenceCount / sessionCount with Excused excluded per BR-05
cases.expected: Module-integration boundary: threshold evaluation persists Notification rows for student and instructor when unexcused absence rate exceeds policy threshold

cases.title: Excused absences excluded from unexcused absence rate numerator per AC-16b
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with Student D enrolled in subject TEST-101
Five completed Closed sessions for Student D: 1 Absent, 1 Excused, 3 Present
Sixth session just closed with Student D Present (6 total completed sessions)
Policy threshold 20%; without Excused exclusion rate would be 2/6 = 33%; with exclusion 1/6 ≈ 17%
steps: Query attendance_records for Student D in TEST-101 where session status Closed
Count records with status Absent (numerator) and total completed sessions (denominator)
Assert Excused records are not included in unexcusedAbsenceCount
Invoke NotificationService.evaluateAbsenceThresholds for the closed session
Query notifications for Student D and assert no AbsenceThresholdWarning created
Change one Present record to Absent via attendance write and re-evaluate; assert notification now created when rate exceeds 20%
cases.expected: AC-16b satisfied: Excused absences excluded from numerator; rate 1/6 does not trigger notification; additional Absent pushes rate above threshold

cases.title: Session Active to Closed transition triggers absence threshold recalculation
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with Active session; attendance_records in Pending state for enrolled students
Student E has prior Closed sessions yielding unexcused rate above 20% threshold
SessionStateGuard allows Active → Closed per state machines §2
AttendanceService.finalizeOnClose configured per API design §5.5 side effects
steps: Assert session beforeState Active and attendance summary pending > 0
Execute session close transition (AttendanceService.finalizeOnClose: Pending → Absent)
Assert session afterState Closed with closedAt set
Assert attendance summary absent count incremented and pending count zeroed
Verify SessionClosed domain event emitted and consumed by NotificationService
Assert threshold evaluation runs post-close and creates notifications when rate exceeds policy
cases.expected: State transition Active → Closed finalizes attendance counts and triggers absence threshold evaluation per AC-16a Given/When/Then and WF-06

cases.title: Tag-specific integration: session monitor poll returns updated present/absent counts after close
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with Active session and 10 enrolled students; 8 Present, 2 Pending
Student F among Pending with prior 2 Absent of 4 completed sessions in subject (40% before close)
Session close will add third Absent for Student F (3/5 = 60%)
steps: GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/attendance before close and record summary.absent baseline
POST /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/close and await finalizeOnClose commit
GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/attendance and assert summary.absent === baseline + 2 (Pending → Absent)
Assert Student F record status Absent in records array
Run NotificationService.evaluateAbsenceThresholds and assert notification created using updated absent count
Verify unexcusedAbsenceCount in notification payload matches DB count of Absent (not Excused) for closed sessions
cases.expected: Tag-specific integration hint satisfied: session attendance poll returns updated present/absent counts after close that drive threshold recalculation

cases.title: GET /notifications returns 200 JSON list with AbsenceThresholdWarning payload fields
preconditions: Student G with existing AbsenceThresholdWarning notification seeded after threshold exceeded
Authenticated Student G session with notification:read (self) permission
steps: Send GET /api/v1/notifications with Student G session cookie
Assert HTTP status 200 OK
Assert Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8
Assert response body contains items array per API design §10.1 success list envelope
Assert at least one item has type AbsenceThresholdWarning with payload containing subjectCode, subjectName, absenceRate, threshold, sessionCount, unexcusedAbsenceCount per domain model §4.13
Assert readAt is null for unread notification and createdAt is ISO 8601 UTC
PATCH /api/v1/notifications/:notificationId/read and assert HTTP 204; re-GET and assert readAt populated
cases.expected: HTTP 200 success contract for GET /notifications and PATCH read per API design §9.1–§9.2; AbsenceThresholdWarning DTO complete

cases.title: PUT /policy/absence-threshold returns 200 when admin sets valid thresholdPercent
preconditions: Authenticated TrainingOfficeAdmin session with policy:write permission
Default policy absence_threshold_percent = 20 seeded
steps: GET /api/v1/policy/absence-threshold using Admin session and record current thresholdPercent
Send PUT /api/v1/policy/absence-threshold with body { "thresholdPercent": 25 } and Admin session cookie
Assert HTTP status 200 OK
Assert response body thresholdPercent === 25
GET /api/v1/policy/absence-threshold and assert persisted value 25
Restore PUT with thresholdPercent 20 for test isolation
cases.expected: HTTP contract for admin policy configuration per API design §9.3; threshold persisted in PolicySetting

cases.title: Instructor denied PUT /policy/absence-threshold with 403 Forbidden
preconditions: Authenticated Instructor session without policy:write per roles-permissions §2.2
Default absence threshold policy exists
steps: Send PUT /api/v1/policy/absence-threshold with body { "thresholdPercent": 15 } using Instructor session cookie
Assert HTTP status 403 Forbidden
Assert response errorCode Forbidden
Assert message Bạn không có quyền thực hiện thao tác này per roles-permissions §2.5
GET /api/v1/policy/absence-threshold using Instructor session and assert HTTP 403 Forbidden with errorCode Forbidden per roles-permissions §2.2
Verify policy value unchanged in database (still 20)
cases.expected: Only TrainingOfficeAdmin holds policy:write; instructor threshold configuration denied per RBAC matrix

cases.title: Student GET /notifications returns only self-scoped notifications with no peer data
preconditions: Student H and Student I enrolled in same subject; Student I has AbsenceThresholdWarning notification
Student H has no threshold notifications
Both students authenticated with notification:read (self)
steps: GET /api/v1/notifications using Student H session cookie
Assert HTTP 200 and items array contains no notification with userId or payload referencing Student I
GET /api/v1/notifications using Student I session cookie
Assert HTTP 200 and items contain Student I's AbsenceThresholdWarning only
Attempt PATCH /api/v1/notifications/:studentINotificationId/read as Student H
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden; Student I notification readAt remains null
cases.expected: Notifications scoped to authenticated user; students cannot read or mark peer absence warnings per notification:read (self)

cases.title: No notification at exactly 20% unexcused rate; notification fires above 20% boundary
preconditions: Student J enrolled in subject with policy threshold 20%
Scenario A: 10 completed Closed sessions with exactly 2 Absent (rate = 0.20, not > 0.20 per BR-05 condition)
Scenario B: 10 completed Closed sessions with 3 Absent (rate = 0.30 > 0.20)
FR-16 enabled
steps: For Scenario A: close eleventh session without adding new Absent for Student J; run threshold evaluation
GET /api/v1/notifications for Student J and assert no AbsenceThresholdWarning (rate exactly 20% does not exceed threshold)
For Scenario B: with 3 Absent of 10 completed, close next session adding fourth Absent (4/11 ≈ 36%)
Run threshold evaluation after close
GET /api/v1/notifications for Student J and assert AbsenceThresholdWarning created
Assert payload.absenceRate > payload.threshold (0.20)
cases.expected: BR-05 boundary: condition requires rate strictly greater than 20%; at exactly 20% no notification; above 20% notification fires

cases.title: PUT /policy/absence-threshold with thresholdPercent 0 or 101 returns 422 ValidationFailed
preconditions: Authenticated TrainingOfficeAdmin session
Validation rules §3.9: thresholdPercent integer 1–100
steps: PUT /api/v1/policy/absence-threshold with body { "thresholdPercent": 0 } using Admin session
Assert HTTP status 422 Unprocessable Entity
Assert errorCode ValidationFailed with field detail for thresholdPercent
PUT /api/v1/policy/absence-threshold with body { "thresholdPercent": 101 }
Assert HTTP 422 ValidationFailed
Verify policy setting unchanged at default 20
cases.expected: Invalid policy threshold boundaries rejected with ValidationFailed; policy unchanged per validation rules §3.9

cases.title: No notification when unexcused absence rate remains at or below 20% after session close
preconditions: Student K with 8 completed Closed sessions: 7 Present, 1 Absent (12.5% rate)
Ninth session closed with Student K Present
FR-16 enabled; threshold 20%
steps: POST /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/close for ninth session
Await threshold evaluation job
GET /api/v1/notifications for Student K
Assert items array contains no AbsenceThresholdWarning for subject
GET /api/v1/notifications for assigned instructor
Assert no AbsenceThresholdWarning notification for Student K
cases.expected: Below-threshold students receive no absence warning notification after session close per BR-05 condition

cases.title: Student sees in-app absence threshold warning toast and persistent badge on /history
preconditions: Student L authenticated in browser; unexcused rate exceeds 20% after instructor closes session
AbsenceThresholdWarning notification created for Student L
Should-capability FR-16 UI enabled per user flows §11
steps: Trigger session close via API or instructor action so threshold notification is created for Student L
Navigate Student L browser to /history or poll notification bell
Assert in-app toast/banner displays absence warning with subject name and rate per user flows §11 step 1
Navigate to /history and locate affected subject row
Assert persistent warning badge visible on subject with high absence rate per user flows §11 step 2
Tap notification or badge and verify detail shows unexcused count and threshold percentage
cases.expected: AC-16a UI outcome: student receives in-app absence threshold warning with optional persistent badge on affected subject in history

cases.title: Instructor receives in-app notification after closing session that triggers absence threshold for enrolled student
preconditions: Authenticated Instructor on /sessions/:sessionId session detail for Active session
Student M enrolled with unexcused rate above 20% before this session close
Student M will be Absent when session closes (Pending → Absent)
FR-16 Should-capability enabled
steps: Navigate to /sessions/:sessionId and confirm session Active with roster showing Student M Pending
Click Đóng buổi học and confirm close dialog
Assert session status badge transitions to Closed
Wait for notification delivery (poll inbox or toast within reasonable async window)
Assert in-app notification appears for Instructor referencing Student M and subject with absence rate above threshold
Open notification center and verify AbsenceThresholdWarning entry with subjectCode, absenceRate, and student identifier
cases.expected: Instructor receives in-app absence threshold warning when session close recalculates rate above 20% per AC-16a and user flows §11

cases.title: Tag-specific browser-journey: instructor monitor dashboard refreshes within 5 seconds after session close
preconditions: Authenticated Instructor on /sessions/:sessionId Theo dõi or Danh sách tab
Active session with mixed Present and Pending students including threshold-affected student
SessionMonitorDashboard or roster view polls attendance every 5 seconds per AC-15 pattern
steps: Record baseline StatCard or summary absent/present counts on session monitor view
Close session via Đóng buổi học action
Without manual reload, wait up to 5 seconds for roster poll to reflect Closed session state
Assert absent count includes Pending students finalized to Absent
Assert session status indicator shows Closed
Verify absence threshold notification appears for instructor shortly after counts stabilize
cases.expected: Tag-specific browser-journey hint: instructor monitor view refreshes finalized present/absent counts within 5 seconds of session close, enabling threshold notification UX

cases.title: Flow A extension: session close after workshop triggers threshold evaluation when rate exceeded
preconditions: Flow A completed through step 6: roster seeded, session opened, students checked in, instructor closes session
At least one student with cumulative unexcused Absent rate > 20% in subject after close
FR-16 Should-capability enabled
steps: Complete Flow A steps 1–6 per testing-plan §6 including POST /sessions/:id/close
Await NotificationService.evaluateAbsenceThresholds triggered by SessionClosed
GET /api/v1/notifications for affected student
Assert AbsenceThresholdWarning notification present with correct subject and rate payload
GET /api/v1/notifications for session instructor
Assert instructor warning notification references affected student
cases.expected: Flow A lifecycle close step integrates absence threshold notification per WF-06 when Should-capability enabled

cases.title: Draft and Active sessions excluded from completed session denominator
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with Student N: 2 Absent of 4 Closed sessions (50% rate)
One Draft session and one Active session with Absent-equivalent Pending rows exist for same subject
Draft and Active sessions must not count toward sessionCount denominator per BR-05 exceptions
steps: Query completed sessions count for Student N in subject (status Closed only)
Assert sessionCount === 4 excluding Draft and Active sessions
Invoke evaluateAbsenceThresholds after closing a fifth session where Student N is Present
Assert rate calculated as 2/5 = 40% not inflated by non-closed sessions
Assert notification created because 40% > 20%
Verify Draft session attendance status does not affect numerator or denominator
cases.expected: BR-05 scope: only Closed sessions count as completed; Draft and Active excluded from threshold denominator

cases.title: Custom admin policy threshold applied during absence rate evaluation after session close
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with Student O: 2 Absent of 9 completed Closed sessions (22.2% rate before tenth close)
PolicySetting absence_threshold_percent = 25 seeded via PUT /api/v1/policy/absence-threshold
Tenth session just closed with Student O Present (10 completed sessions, 2 Absent = 20% rate)
steps: Assert policy_settings row absence_threshold_percent = 25
Invoke NotificationService.evaluateAbsenceThresholds for closed session
Query notifications for Student O and assert no AbsenceThresholdWarning (20% not greater than 25% threshold)
PUT policy thresholdPercent to 15 via PolicyService or API
Re-invoke evaluateAbsenceThresholds for same session context
Assert AbsenceThresholdWarning notification created because 20% > 15% per FR-16 admin-configured threshold
cases.expected: FR-16 evaluation uses admin-configured policy threshold from PolicySetting, not hardcoded 20%; notification fires only when rate exceeds configured threshold

cases.title: Admin configures absence threshold at /admin/policy via AttendancePolicyForm
preconditions: Authenticated TrainingOfficeAdmin with policy:write per roles-permissions §2.2
FR-16 Should-capability enabled; /admin/policy accessible from admin chrome (Chính sách nav item)
Default policy absence_threshold_percent = 20 seeded
steps: Navigate to /admin/policy from admin hub or Chính sách sidebar item
Assert AttendancePolicyForm displays Ngưỡng vắng (%) field with default 20 and helper text per forms-validation §4.9
Change threshold input to 25
Click Lưu chính sách and assert success toast appears
Verify persisted value via GET /api/v1/policy/absence-threshold or form reload shows 25
Restore threshold to 20 for test isolation
cases.expected: User flows §11 policy configuration satisfied: admin saves absence threshold at /admin/policy; persisted value drives BR-05 evaluation on next session close

cases.title: AttendanceManuallyEdited triggers threshold recalculation with Excused excluded from numerator
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with Student P: 3 Absent of 5 completed Closed sessions in TEST-102 (60% unexcused rate)
AbsenceThresholdWarning notification already created for Student P in TEST-102
One Absent attendance record on a Closed session within instructor 24-hour edit window
AttendanceManuallyEdited event consumer wired per main-workflows §8
steps: PATCH /api/v1/attendance/:recordId changing one Absent record to Excused with note (≥10 characters per validation §3.8)
Assert HTTP 200 and attendance record status Excused with audit log entry
Await NotificationService threshold re-evaluation triggered by AttendanceManuallyEdited event
Assert unexcusedAbsenceCount recalculated as 2 Absent of 5 completed sessions (Excused excluded per AC-16b)
Query notifications for Student P and assert payload absenceRate = 0.40 and unexcusedAbsenceCount = 2 per domain model §4.13
Assert assigned instructor notification payload reflects same updated rate and counts
cases.expected: WF-06 §8: manual attendance edit triggers absence threshold recalculation; AC-16b Excused exclusion applied on post-edit evaluation
